The Muppets (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $88,625,922Details
International Box Office $71,836,030
Worldwide Box Office $160,461,952
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $31,996,610
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $24,738,647
Total $56,735,257
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On vacation in Los Angeles, Walter, the world’s biggest Muppet fan, his brother Gary and Gary's girlfriend, Mary, from Smalltown, USA, discover the nefarious plan of oilman Tex Richman to raze Muppet Studios and drill for the oil recently discovered beneath the Muppets’ former stomping grounds. To stage a telethon and raise the $10 million needed to save the studio, Walter, Mary and Gary help Kermit reunite the Muppets, who have all gone their separate ways: Fozzie now performs with a Reno casino tribute band called the Moopets, Miss Piggy is a plus-size fashion editor at Vogue Paris, Animal is in a Santa Barbara clinic for anger management, and Gonzo is a high-powered plumbing magnate.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$29,239,026 (33.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.03 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$45,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,440 opening theaters/3,440 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $133,626,496

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 23rd, 2011 (Wide) by Walt Disney, released as The Muppets
International Releases: December 2nd, 2011 (Wide) (Brazil)
January 12th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
January 12th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
January 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Indonesia)
February 3rd, 2012 (Wide) (Spain)
Video Release: March 20th, 2012 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some mild rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2188, 9/7/2011)
Running Time: 98 minutes
Franchise: Muppets
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Character's Journey Shown on Map, Gratuitous Cameos, Prologue, Puppets in a Live-Action World, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Coming of Age, Corporate Malfeasance, Dream Sequence, Kidnap, Performing Arts, Relationships Gone Wrong, Road Trip, Robot
Social setting: TV Industry
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

On vacation in Los Angeles, Walter, the world’s biggest Muppet fan, his brother Gary and Gary's girlfriend, Mary, from Smalltown, USA, discover the nefarious plan of oilman Tex Richman to raze Muppet Studios and drill for the oil recently discovered beneath the Muppets’ former stomping grounds. To stage a telethon and raise the $10 million needed to save the studio, Walter, Mary and Gary help Kermit reunite the Muppets, who have all gone their separate ways: Fozzie now performs with a Reno casino tribute band called the Moopets, Miss Piggy is a plus-size fashion editor at Vogue Paris, Animal is in a Santa Barbara clinic for anger management, and Gonzo is a high-powered plumbing magnate.
